title: Question about sync rate and points
display_title: Question about sync rate and points
wikitext:
[]: Question about sync rate and points             Quote Post by MaskedOmega » Sun Apr 17, 2011 6:11 pm Throughout the series it was mentioned about sync rates and them being referred to as percentage, this how much the pilot and the EVA are connecting right? The other part is that sync rate is also referred to as points, is this the same thing as percentage so when they say your sync rate raised 8 points it is saying 8%? Was this ever addressed in the series?

[]: Re: Question about sync rate and points             Quote Post by AshPhoenix » Sun Apr 17, 2011 9:45 pm MaskedOmega wrote: Throughout the series it was mentioned about sync rates and them being referred to as percentage, this how much the pilot and the EVA are connecting right? The other part is that sync rate is also referred to as points, is this the same thing as percentage so when they say your sync rate raised 8 points it is saying 8%? Was this ever addressed in the series?

[]:             Quote Post by theobject » Tue Apr 26, 2011 10:55 pm I don't think it really ever shows each of the pilots' sync ratios explicitly. I think that they said that 100% is where the pilot and Eva have a 1:1 ratio of pain, control, senses, etc. Exceeding 100% results in a higher/more precise control of the Eva, although the threshold 400% mark can only be reached by the berserker mode. Asuka and Shinji set the record for sync ratio during their first encounter, when they are both in Unit 02 fighting Gaghiel. That number is never mentioned, either, but I would assume it's above 100%, but only slightly so. So yeah, in the end though, I don't know anything. Sorry.

[]: about the sync ratios             Quote Post by Sik » Thu Apr 28, 2011 8:04 pm in the series they never mention exactly how the ratio works or how it affects the pilots. But i dare to suggest thay maybe till 100% it reffers to the pilots body/nervs and beyond that it refers to his/her soul. Any other ideas?
